data.title: Dream Office REIT announces February 2026 monthly distribution of 8.333 cents per REIT Unit. Payable on March 13, 2026.
data.description: In Toronto, Dream Office REIT (TSX: D.UN) revealed its February 2026 monthly distribution of 8.333 cents per REIT Unit, Series A, equivalent to $1.00 on an annual basis. This distribution will be paid out on March 13, 2026, to unitholders listed as of February 27, 2026. As an unincorporated, open-ended real estate investment trust, Dream Office REIT is a leading office landlord situated in downtown Toronto with a portfolio encompassing over 4.0 million square feet.

data.title: Heritage Distilling Holding Co. renamed IP Strategy Holdings Inc. on Feb 18, 2026 (Nasdaq: IPST) - GlobeNewswire.
data.description: On February 18, 2026, IP Strategy Holdings, Inc. (Nasdaq: IPST) revealed its name change from Heritage Distilling Holding Company, Inc. The switch officially took place at 12:01 a.m. Eastern Time on the same day. This move signifies a strategic shift for the company, aiming to redefine its identity and market positioning. The transition represents a pivotal moment for IP Strategy, as it moves forward under the new name to drive growth and innovation within the industry. This development aligns with the company's vision for the future and underscores its commitment to adapt to evolving market dynamics.

data.title: Treasury bond auction announcement for RIKB 28 1115 and RIKB 38 0215, with payment deadline at 14:00 on Settlement Date.
data.description: The Government Debt Management will conduct an auction of Treasury bonds in the Series on the Auction Date between 10:30 am and 11:00 am. The bonds will have specific ISIN numbers and Maturity Dates as per the provided table. Payment for the Treasury bonds must reach the Central Bank by 14:00 on the Settlement Date. The Bonds will be electronically delivered on the same day. Buyers have the option to purchase an additional 10% as per Article 6 of the General Terms of Auction for Treasury bonds.

data.title: Hope For The Warriors gets $3 million grant for programs, infrastructure upgrades, and access to care.
data.description: Hope For The Warriors (HOPE) based in Springfield, Virginia, has been granted $3,000,000 by Lilly Endowment Inc. This funding aims to bolster leadership and workforce development within the organization. The grant will also be utilized for significant upgrades to operational systems and infrastructure supporting HOPE's programs nationwide. Another key focus will be on enhancing access to evidence-based care. Lilly Endowment is providing 18 grants to military and veterans service organizations to strengthen their ability to provide support.

data.title: Billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller shifts investments to Brazil, buys 3.55M EWZ shares worth $113M, exits Big Tech.
data.description: Renowned trader Stanley Druckenmiller is adjusting his investment strategy with a new focus on Brazilian assets. According to the latest 13F filing from his Duquesne Family Office, Druckenmiller has made a significant shift in his portfolio by reducing exposure to popular Silicon Valley stocks and increasing his position in the iShares MSCI Brazil ETF (NYSE: EWZ). The filing shows that Druckenmiller has purchased approximately 3.55 million shares of EWZ, amounting to over $113 million and ranking it as a top-10 holding. In addition to acquiring shares, Druckenmiller has also invested in EWZ call options, indicating a high level of confidence in a potential market rally. This strategic move aligns with Druckenmiller's reputation for anticipating market trends early. For more information, visit <website>.

data.title: Dream Industrial REIT (TSX: DIR.UN) announces February 2026 distribution of 5.833 cents per Unit (70 cents annualized) payable on March 13 to unitholders of record as at February 27. Global portfolio as of December 31, 2025.
data.description: Dream Industrial REIT (TSX: DIR.UN) disclosed its February 2026 monthly distribution at 5.833 cents per Unit (equivalent to 70 cents annually), payable on March 13, 2026 to unitholders recorded by February 27, 2026. By December 31, 2025, the Trust held a stake in a varied global portfolio of strategically situated industrial properties.

data.title: Groundworks® acquires WTX Foundation Repair in West Texas and New Mexico, marking its first expansion into NM.
data.description: In Virginia Beach, Virginia, Groundworks® has disclosed its acquisition of WTX Foundation Repair, a reputable company in Amarillo, Texas. WTX Foundation Repair serves communities in West Texas and Albuquerque, New Mexico. This acquisition is significant as it signifies Groundworks' initial venture into New Mexico and further expands its presence in the Southwest region. Groundworks is recognized as North America's primary foundation and water management solutions company and is experiencing rapid growth.

data.title: Booking Holdings expected to report higher Q4 revenue. Analysts forecast earnings of $48.67 per share, up from $41.55. Revenue estimated at $6.13 billion, up from $5.47 billion in the previous year. CEO of KAYAK named. Shares closed at $4,140.15.
data.description: Booking Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: BKNG) is set to announce its Q4 earnings after market close on Feb. 18. Analysts predict earnings of $48.67 per share for the Norwalk-based company, a rise from $41.55 per share in the previous year. The consensus revenue estimate for the quarter is $6.13 billion, up from $5.47 billion in the same period last year. Peer Bueller was recently appointed as the CEO of KAYAK under Booking Holdings on Feb. 3. Booking Holdings stock closed at $4,140.15, a 0.01% drop, on Tuesday. For more details and the latest analyst ratings, readers can visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga Pro. Check out how Benzinga's top analysts have rated the company recently on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Cosmos Health's C-Scrub is now at Tesco, UK's largest retailer with ~30% market share, showing growing momentum.
data.description: Tesco, the leading retailer in the UK with a market share of nearly 30%, has started carrying Cosmos Health's C-Scrub, an antimicrobial skin cleanser.

data.title: Banqup and Visa announce strategic partnership for e-invoicing and e-payment solutions, involving Banqup SA and Visa.
data.description: In La Hulpe, Belgium on February 18, 2026, Banqup SA (traded on Euronext as BANQ) revealed a significant strategic alliance with Visa (listed on NYSE as V), a prominent player in digital payments. The collaboration focuses on Visa's Direct and Commercial Solutions branches, strengthening Banqup's position as a top European provider for business management, e-invoicing, and payment automation.

data.title: Westlake Royal launches widest vinyl Board & Batten profile – 9” wide, expanding Royal® & Exterior Portfolio® lines.
data.description: In Houston, Westlake Royal Building Products introduced a new 9” wide vinyl Board & Batten profile in their Royal® and Exterior Portfolio® lines. This broadest vinyl Board & Batten profile currently available in the market signifies the trend towards wider siding options for enhanced curb appeal in modern settings. Westlake, a prominent company listed on NYSE as WLK, aims to cater to the evolving preferences for larger-scale aesthetics in home exteriors.

data.title: Peter Thiel-backed ETHZilla went from $107 to $3, with a 97% collapse. They sold their 7.5% stake in August 2025 after holding over 100,000 ETH tokens.
data.description: Billionaire Peter Thiel and Founders Fund recently made headlines for their decision to divest completely from ETHZilla (NASDAQ: ETHZ). The duo initially purchased a 7.5% stake in the company back in August 2025, when shares were at $107. However, this investment took a drastic turn, with shares plummeting to $3, marking a 97% decrease. This shift transformed what was initially a prominent Ethereum treasury play into a cautionary tale. The company's pivot from a biotech firm to an Ethereum treasury entity initially fuelled a 90% surge in share prices in a single trading session. Nonetheless, the timing coincided with a challenging period for the cryptocurrency market, resulting in ETHZilla liquidating a significant portion of its assets. In October, the company sold around $40 million worth of ETH after the crypto market crash. Notably, ETHZilla had amassed more than 100,000 ETH tokens following a successful $565 million fundraising round involving Electric Capital, Polychain Capital, and GSR. For additional details, the full article can be found on Benzinga.com.
